 You will join a collaborative People Services & Operations team that supports more than 130,000 colleagues worldwide. This is a unique opportunity to shape futureproof benefit operations through automation, digitalisation, and structured operational governance.
   

  





**Key responsibilities include:**



* Deliver and oversee operational benefit processes in a global context, ensuring accuracy, compliance, and a highquality employee experience.
* Own the operational governance for selected benefit areas, including oversight of core data, operational rules, and endtoend process design, as well as coordination with key stakeholders across markets and functions.
* Establish and maintain clear routines, documentation, and process standards, enabling consistent and reliable execution across the team.
* Ensure high data quality and secure data flows between HR systems, payroll, and external vendors, applying strong analytical and Excelbased problemsolving skills.
* Drive improvements and automation, identifying opportunities to simplify, standardise, and digitalise benefit operations over time.
* Collaborate widely across the organisation, working closely with Payroll, Rewards, Tech, local HR teams, and external partners to ensure aligned and efficient delivery.
* Provide expert Tier 2–3 support on complex benefit questions and operational topics, offering clarity and guidance in challenging cases.



  

  





**WHO YOU ARE​**
  

 You thrive in a role where hands on operations meet continuous improvement, and you bring the skills and mindset needed to navigate a complex, global benefit environment. You combine analytical strength with structured execution and clear communication. Specifically, you bring:
   

  




* Strong analytical ability and expert level Excel skills (complex logic; VBA is a plus).
* Confidence working with complex, multi-country data and sensitive financial information.
* Clear, adaptable communication with stakeholders at different levels and across many markets.
* The ability to bring structure to ambiguous or evolving operational landscapes.
* Experience within HR operations, benefits administration, payroll operations, or similar fields.
* A Bachelor degree in Human Resources, Business administration or related field.
* Fluency in English and Swedish.
* Experience working with operational controls, audit requirements, or risk-based process governance is a strong plus.



  

  





**WHO WE ARE​**




 H&M Group is a global company of strong fashion brands and ventures. Our goal is to prove that there is no compromise between exceptional design, affordable prices, and sustainable solutions. We want to liberate fashion for the many, and our customers are at the heart of every decision we make.​
   

  





 We are made up of thousands of passionate and talented colleagues united by our shared culture and values. Together, we want to use our power, our scale, and our knowledge to push the fashion industry towards a more inclusive and sustainable future. ​
   

  





  

  





**WHY YOU’LL LOVE WORKING HERE ​**




 You’ll be part of a team that works closely together, supports each other, and drives progress through collaboration. The role gives you the opportunity to work in a global setting while helping us modernise and streamline how we deliver benefits. You’ll have real impact on creating simpler, smarter operations for the future.
   

  





 At H&M Group, we are proud to be a vibrant and welcoming company. We offer our employees attractive benefits with extensive development opportunities around the globe.
   

  

  

  

 An example of our benefits:
   

  




* 25% Staff discount on all our H&M Group brands, both in stores and online (H&M, COS, Weekday, Monki, H&M HOME, & Other Stories and ARKET).
* H&M Incentive Program – HIP. Learn more about the program here.
* With a presence in markets around the world, we offer extensive career development and international mobility.



  

  





**JOIN US​**




 Our uniqueness comes from a combination of many things – our inclusive and collaborative culture, our strong values, and opportunities for growth. But most of all, it’s our people who make us who we are.​
   

  





 Take the next step in your career together with us. The journey starts here.​
   

  





 ​\*We are committed to a recruitment process that is fair, equitable, and based on competency. We therefore kindly ask you to not attach a cover letter in your application.​

title: Junior Android Developer
description:
Marshall Group is the audio, tech, and design powerhouse uniting musicians and music lovers through genre-breaking innovation. Our flagship brand, Marshall, is uniquely positioned with over 60 years of rock ‘n’ roll attitude on stage, at home, and on the go. Our iconic products are brought to life by a dedicated team of 800 passionate employees and sold in over 90 markets worldwide.
   

  

 Right now, we need to strengthen our Software team with an Android developer that will be working with our cross-functional teams. The world of audio is changing. Smart speakers and True Wireless headphones are turning audio into a high-tech industry. In the last few years, our products have gone from simple wired devices to connected and complex IoT devices. We are now focusing on evolving our mobile app and the added value it can provide to our users.
   

  

**What you’ll do:**
* Maintain and develop Android mobile apps for the Marshall brands.
* Collaborate closely with our UX and Design team to discuss new features and functions.
* Support and participate in testing activities and evaluation of the app.
* Participate in knowledge-sharing sessions to promote new ideas and innovation.
* Teamwork with the other developers to help us brainstorm solutions and make our apps even better for our users.
* Prototyping new ideas to make them come true, both individually and across teams.


**Who we’re looking for:**
 You have a can-do mindset and cooperate well internally and externally. You're humble enough to know there's always more to learn, and curious enough to actually enjoy that process. There will be plenty of both here. This role sits at the intersection of audio heritage and fast-moving tech, so you'll need to be comfortable picking things up quickly, asking good questions, and diving in.
   

  

 To succeed, you need to be committed to collaborative problem-solving and building quality products that create great user experiences together with the UX and design team. We believe you have a great passion for new technologies, trends, and software development. You get excited by the idea of creating an amazing app experience that heightens the experience of the user.
   

  

**You probably have the following experiences & skills:**
* Proven experience of Android app development (We're looking for someone fairly early in their career.)
* Experience working with Kotlin.
* Proficient in modern android design patterns (for ex. MVVM, dependency injection) and Jetpack components.
* Familiarity with coroutines and Compose, Dagger Hilt and Clean Architecture.
* Familiarity with Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) would be very welcome but is not a must


**Our pledge:**
 We thrive to foster an inclusive workplace and we do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, disability, colour, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age or marital status. We firmly believe that Marshall thrives when our employees do, leading to better experiences for our consumers.

title: Senior regionjurist med fokus på dataskydd, IT-rätt och AI-juridik
description:
**Är du samhällsintresserad och vill arbeta i demokratins tjänst med omväxlande juridiska frågor som har betydelse för invånarna i Region Stockholm? Då kan tjänsten som senior regionjurist med fokus på dataskydd, IT-rätt och AI-juridik vara rätt för dig!**
 Om oss
   

  

 Region Stockholm är en av Sveriges största arbetsgivare – en organisation med cirka 50 000 anställda och en budget för 2026 om 140 miljarder kronor. Våra kärnuppdrag är hälso- och sjukvård, kollektivtrafik, regional utveckling samt kultur. Årligen genomförs 250–300 upphandlingar till ett totalt värde av cirka 30 miljarder kronor och Region Stockholm är därigenom även en av Sveriges största inköpsorganisationer.
   

  

 Regionledningskontoret är regionstyrelsens förvaltning. Kontorets uppdrag är att styra, utveckla, följa upp och samordna Region Stockholms verksamhet utifrån regionfullmäktiges och regionstyrelsens beslut.
   

  

 Det finns två juridiska enheter på regionledningskontoret, dels Kommunal- och förvaltningsrätt, dels Upphandling och affärsjuridik och båda har till uppgift att ge kvalificerad juridisk rådgivning och service till både politiker och tjänstemän i Region Stockholms verksamheter. Enheten Kommunal- och förvaltningsrätts arbete spänner över många olika rättsområden såsom kommunal- och förvaltningsrätt, hälso- och sjukvårdsrätt, offentlighet och sekretess, IT-rätt och AI-juridik, dataskydd samt totalförsvarsrätt.
   

  

 Om tjänsten
   

  

 Som senior regionjurist på enheten för Kommunal- och förvaltningsrätt med fokus på dataskydd, IT-rätt och AI-juridik kommer arbetet i stor utsträckning att handla om att ge expertstöd inom dataskydd samt offentlighet och sekretess, ofta med koppling till hälso- och sjukvård, medicinsk forskning och upphandling. Du kommer även att vara en viktig del i regionens utvecklingsarbete på AI-området. Arbete inom enhetens övriga rättsområden förekommer också.
   

  

 Arbetet är omväxlande och präglas av många kontaktytor inom hela Region Stockholm och du kommer även ha en viktig roll i att representera Region Stockholm i olika nätverk med bland annat andra regioner och Sveriges Kommuner och Regioner (SKR) i frågor om dataskydd och AI-utveckling.
   

  

 Vem är du?
   

  

 Vi söker dig som har gedigen erfarenhet av kvalificerat juridiskt arbete i offentlig verksamhet. Du har många års erfarenhet av arbete med tillämpning av dataskyddslagstiftning och IT-rätt gärna med anknytning till hälso- och sjukvård och näraliggande områden såsom medicinsk forskning och/eller socialtjänst. Erfarenhet av arbete med andra hälso- och sjukvårdsjuridiska frågor, offentlighet och sekretess samt AI-juridik är meriterande.
   

  

 Som person är du lösningsorienterad, analytisk och kommunikativ med förmåga att skapa goda relationer. Du är dessutom ansvarsfull och självgående med stor integritet och förmåga att utifrån verksamhetens behov driva och följa upp det juridiska arbetet. Verksamheten är i stor utsträckning omvärldsstyrd och du behöver ha stor flexibilitet då fokus kan ändras snabbt.
   

  

 Du har en god förmåga till avancerad rättslig analys och argumentation. Du är kreativ och har lätt för att bryta ner komplexa problem och sätta in frågor i ett större perspektiv. Du kan på ett lättbegripligt och pedagogiskt sätt förmedla relevant kunskap och juridisk rådgivning till såväl jurister som icke-jurister.
   

  

 Vi lägger stor vikt vid dina personliga egenskaper.
   

  

**För Att Passa För Rollen Har Du**
* Juristexamen (motsvarande tidigare jur.kand).
* Mångårig yrkeserfarenhet av kvalificerat juridiskt arbete i offentlig verksamhet med fokus på dataskydd och IT-rätt.


 Det är meriterande om du dessutom har erfarenhet av flera av de rättsområden som enheten arbetar med, särskilt hälso- och sjukvårdsrätt, offentlighet och sekretess samt AI-juridik. Erfarenhet av arbete i domstol och/eller i en politiskt styrd organisation är också meriterande.
   

  

 Vårt erbjudande
   

  

 Vi har kollektivavtal och med det följer kollektivavtalade försäkringar, tjänstepension och möjlighet till löneväxling. För att du ska kunna ta ansvar för din hälsa erbjuder vi friskvårdstimme och friskvårdsbidrag samt subventionerad sjukvård i form av kostnadsfri öppensjukvård. Flexibel arbetstid och möjlighet till att arbeta hemifrån har vi för att underlätta för dig att få ihop vardagen. Här kan du läsa mer om förmånerna i vårt kollektivavtal.
   

  

 Vi sitter i trevliga lokaler på Lindhagensgatan 98. Hit tar du dig enkelt med kollektivtrafiken.
   

  

 Ansökan
   

  

 Låter det här som något för dig ser vi gärna att du söker så snart som möjligt, intervjuer sker löpande. För en komplett ansökan bifogas CV och personligt brev.
   

  

 Har du frågor är du välkommen att kontakta enhetschef för Kommunal- och förvaltningsrätt, Hanna Nyström Schraml: hanna.nystrom-schraml@regionstockholm.se.
   

  

 Varmt välkommen med din ansökan!
   

  

 Regionledningskontoret stödjer regionstyrelsen i uppgiften att leda, styra, samordna och följa upp Region Stockholms verksamheter. Förvaltningen arbetar strategiskt och operativt utifrån ett koncernperspektiv och ger regionstyrelsen de underlag och det stöd den behöver i sitt uppdrag att utveckla Region Stockholm. Regionledningskontoret ansvarar även för stöd till klimat- och regionutvecklingsnämnden samt för verksamhetsstödjande tjänster inom koncernen. Läs mer här
   

  

**Att söka jobb i Region Stockholm**
 Vi eftersträvar jämställdhet och jämlikhet på vår arbetsplats och ser gärna sökande med olika bakgrund och förutsättningar.
   

  

 Vi tar endast emot ansökningar via detta system. Ansök genom att klicka på knappen ”Ansök”. Ansökningar per brev eller e-post beaktas inte. Vi undanber oss direktkontakt med bemannings- och rekryteringsföretag samt säljare av ytterligare jobbannonser.
   

  

 Vårt rekryteringssystem kan inte hantera anonyma ansökningar eller sökande med skyddade personuppgifter. Om du har skyddade personuppgifter ber vi dig att kontakta den kontaktpersonen som finns angiven i annonsen. Din ansökan kommer då att hanteras utanför rekryteringssystemet. Du bör även vara försiktig med vilken information du lämnar i din ansökan och endast ta med information som är relevant för den aktuella befattningen.
   

  

 Region Stockholm ansvarar för hälso- och sjukvård, kollektivtrafik, regional utveckling och bidrar till kulturlivet. Varje dag, dygnet runt. I landets snabbaste växande region. Tillsammans skapar vi Europas attraktivaste storstadsregion.
   

  

 Läs mer om hur det är att jobba hos oss i Region Stockholm
   

  

 Anställningsform Tillsvidareanställning Anställningens omfattning Heltid Tillträde Enligt överenskommelse Löneform Månadslön Antal lediga befattningar 1 Sysselsättningsgrad 100% Ort Stockholm Län Stockholms län Land Sverige Referensnummer 2026/3830 Kontakt Hanna Nyström Schraml, enhetschef, +46707374904 Facklig företrädare Jonas Ivarsson, SACO, 08-12314458Tommy Mäntynen, Vision, tommy.mantynen@regionstockholm.seThérèse Juvall, Akademikerförbundet SSR, 08-12313942 Publicerat 2026-05-28 Sista ansökningsdag 2026-06-14

title: Product Marketing Manager
description:
****Join us to shape the future!****
 Teamtailor is a global Employer Branding and ATS SaaS platform used by over 12,000 companies, 250,000 users, and available in 90 countries worldwide. 🌍
   

  

 Working at Teamtailor means being part of a dynamic, fast-paced tech company that values impact and responsibility. Our workplace fosters an environment where everyone can contribute meaningfully to the growth of the company. 🎉🥳
   

  

 Building a diverse team with a wide range of experiences, skills, and backgrounds has always been central to Teamtailor’s mission, and it remains a strength today. We welcome all individuals who share our vision and are committed to contributing to our collective success. Join us in helping companies and people communicate in more meaningful ways to make life-changing decisions together.👫
   

  

 We’re now looking for a
 **Product Marketing Manager** 
 to help us define how we communicate, package, position, and grow our product offering across every customer touchpoint.
   

  

 This is a highly cross-functional role at the intersection of product, marketing, sales and customer success.
   

  

 You’ll help shape how Teamtailor brings products, features, and value to market, while ensuring customers clearly understand not only what we build, but why it matters.
   

  

 You’ll play a key role in driving product adoption, feature awareness, customer understanding, positioning and growth. And help elevate how Teamtailor communicates its product experience across the entire customer journey.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
**Define product positioning and messaging!**
* Own how Teamtailor communicates its product offering across all touchpoints
* Develop clear, compelling positioning and messaging for products, features, and use cases
* Ensure consistency in how we talk about our value proposition globally
* Continuously refine how we package and present our offering to different audiences and segments


**Drive product adoption and feature awareness!**
* Build strategies and campaigns that increase feature discovery, understanding, and adoption
* Help customers understand the value of both existing and new product capabilities
* Partner closely with Customer Success and Product teams to improve adoption journeys and customer education
* Support go-to-market initiatives for launches, updates, and strategic product initiatives


**Elevate how we package and sell the product!**
* Improve how Teamtailor’s product offering is presented across:
+ website
+ campaigns
+ sales material
+ onboarding
+ lifecycle communication
+ product launches
+ customer communication

* Help simplify complex functionality into clear customer value
* Ensure our product story feels modern, differentiated, and commercially strong


**Collaborate across teams!**
* Work closely with Product teams to deeply understand customer problems, product strategy, and roadmap priorities
* Partner with Brand & Creative to bring positioning and launches to life in compelling ways
* Collaborate with Performance Marketing to turn positioning into scalable acquisition and growth initiatives
* Support Sales with messaging, enablement, and customer-facing narratives
* Partner with Customer Success to understand customer needs, friction points, and adoption opportunities


**Own customer and market insights!**
* Become the voice of the customer internally
* Gather and synthesize customer insights, feedback, market trends, and competitive intelligence
* Help inform and inspire Product teams around customer needs and opportunities
* Identify gaps in customer understanding, positioning, or adoption and turn them into actionable initiatives


**Bring a modern and analytical mindset!**
* Use data and insights to evaluate adoption, engagement, and positioning effectiveness
* Stay curious about how AI and modern tools are changing product marketing, communication, and customer education
* Explore smarter and more scalable ways to create customer understanding and product engagement


**Who you are!**
 You’re a strategic thinker with strong commercial instincts and a passion for connecting products with people.
   

  

**You Know How To**
* simplify complexity
* tell compelling product stories
* understand customer needs
* turn positioning into growth


**You Likely Have**
* Experience in Product Marketing, Growth Marketing, or a similar strategic marketing role
* Experience working cross-functionally with Product, Sales, Customer Success, and Marketing teams
* Strong understanding of SaaS products and customer journeys
* Excellent communication and storytelling skills
* A strong commercial mindset and understanding of how product communication drives growth and adoption
* Analytical thinking and comfort working with customer insights and product data
* The ability to balance strategic thinking with hands-on execution
* Curiosity around AI, modern marketing workflows, and evolving customer behavior


**Bonus If You**
* Have experience from SaaS or HR tech
* Have worked in scaling international B2B companies
* Have experience driving product adoption or onboarding initiatives
* Have strong instincts for positioning and packaging products in competitive markets


 If this sounds like you, please click apply for this job or reach out to our TA team

title: YPP, Human Resources (People Management & Development)
Head of Value Engineering - Nordics
company: Process Analytics Factory - PAFnow by Celonis
description:
Celonis is the global leader in Process Intelligence and the pioneer of Process Mining technology. As one of the world’s fastest-growing enterprise SaaS companies, we are changemakers pushing the boundaries of what’s possible. We invest heavily in advanced AI capabilities—specifically our Process Intelligence Graph—to turn data insights into immediate business action. We believe there is a massive opportunity to unlock global productivity and sustainability by placing intelligence at the core of every business process. Join our mission to make processes work for people, companies, and the planet.
   

  

**The Team**
 The Celonis
 **Value Engineering (VE)** 
 team is focused on managing the entire customer value lifecycle, from sales through value realization, renewal and expansion. As a
 **Head of Value Engineering** 
 , you will lead a dynamic team who come from a variety of backgrounds to identify, frame and deliver value to our customers.
   

  

**The Role**
 As the Head of Value Engineering for the Nordics, you will lead a team of Value Engineers and drive end-to-end customer value journeys, landing and expanding existing customers (ACV growth) and renewals (renewal rate). You will work closely with the Celonis GTM Leadership Teams and be responsible for the end-to-end value journey for all customers within your region/vertical, supporting customers to achieve their strategic goals and realize significant value using Celonis Process Intelligence Platform. You are a trusted advisor to the Celonis Executive Team, supporting their engagements with strategic customers and senior stakeholders.
   

  

 As a leader in one of the fastest-growing tech companies globally, you will bring together deep process and industry knowledge with a proven consulting skill set and strong analytical abilities. You will foster a positive and supportive work environment for your teams, promoting employee satisfaction and career growth.
   

  

**The Work You’ll Do**
**Identify & Frame Value For Prospects/Customers**
* Own the end-to-end customer value journey, including responsibility for landing, expanding, adoption and renewing accounts
* Translate customers’ strategic objectives into high-impact Celonis use cases and evangelize Process Intelligence across prospects and customers
* Communicate the value opportunity and strategic roadmap to C-Level executives in partnership with sales leadership and Celonis executives
* Serve as an executive sponsor across the customer portfolio and solidify Celonis as a strategic business transformation platform for our customers


**Create & Scale Value Across The Portfolio**
* Lead customers through the Celonis Value Journey, support the deployment of new features, apps, and products across the customer base, and advance strategic growth programs in pursuit of value creation
* Ensure customer success and architect improvement measures and manage change within the customer base, while pioneering new methodologies within Celonis’ Value Engineering organisation
* Direct Celonis Value Engineering, Services and Ecosystem resources through the Celonis Customer Value Journey, creating durable value for our customers
* Take ownership and build strategic customer relationships as an executive sponsor
* Host QBRs (Quarterly Business Reviews) and EBRs (Executive Business Reviews) to communicate results and realized value to senior management and C-level executives


**Grow & Lead Celonis Talent**
* Recruit the next-generation of Celonis Value Engineers, while setting a high-standard for candidate excellence
* Develop world-class talent through hands-on engagement, continuous feedback, and active mentorship
* Lead by example and consistently demonstrate the Celonis Leadership Principles (lead with purpose, facilitate greatness, embrace difference, and cultivate balance)
* Effectively allocate Celonis resources to deliver on company sales, renewals, value, and adoption targets
* Communicate success and risks proactively within the organization


**The Qualifications You Need**
* Domain Expertise: Demonstrate your business domain expertise, such as in Finance or Supply Chain as well as experience in strategic transformation initiatives (e.g. Shared Services Transformation or System Transformation) and ability to build a compelling value roadmap for the customer
* Industry Expertise: Demonstrate a deep understanding of industry trends and strategic challenges of the C-Suite and build an integrated Celonis Process Intelligence roadmap
* Product Expertise: Experience with guiding customer’s IT teams throughout their Celonis Process Intelligence journey, demonstrating hands-on the power of Process Intelligence and proven ability to accelerate value realization by building dashboards, Apps, Action Flows
* Project Management: Plan and manage project scope, expectations and timelines. Leverage partners from the Celonis Ecosystem wherever possible.
* Executive Stakeholder Management: Experience in preparing and communicating results to stakeholders and management, both internally and externally.
* Value Selling Methodology: Demonstrate experience of following the Celonis value selling methodology alongside the customer journey: including discovery, qualification, value assessment and PoV’s, and demonstrate the full value potential of Celonis Process Intelligence
* Value Realization: Proven track record of identifying and prioritizing use cases, implementing improvement measures and becoming a change agent for the customer by establishing an operating model and training users for the customer to realize value and renew/expand their subscription with Celonis
* Sales & Commercial Acumen: Demonstrate ability to qualify all VE engagements, do discovery with customers directly, steer/allocate resources efficiently, and champion our value selling methodology with our customers.


***Leadership Skills:** 
 Proven ability to lead by example, including being on the front line with your teams, hands-on in all aspects and a role model for the organization.*
**What Celonis Can Offer You**
* Pioneer Innovation: Work with the global leader in Process Mining and the Process Intelligence Graph to shape the future of AI-driven business operations.
* Ownership from Day 1: Every full-time "Celonaut" is an owner, receiving Restricted Stock Units (RSUs) and merit-based refresh grants.
* Unrivaled Family Support: Benefit from our inclusive parental leave policy—24 weeks of fully paid leave for primary carers and 12 weeks for supporting carers, available from your first day of employment.
* Work-Life Integration: Enjoy Unlimited PTO (in applicable regions) and generous PTO globally, as well as a flexible hybrid work model that balances remote focus with vibrant office collaboration.
* Continuous Growth: Elevate your skills through our 70-20-10 learning framework, mentorship programs, and access to a dedicated learning platform.
* Holistic Well-being: Prioritize your health with subsidized Wellhub memberships, mental health counseling, and dedicated "Wellness Weeks" that prioritize work/life balance.
* Drive Sustainability: Participate in annual Impact Days, where you receive paid time off to volunteer for community and environmental causes with your local office, or virtually.
* Global Inclusion & Belonging: Find community through our Inclusion Think Tank and participate in our annual Inclusion Days, ensuring every voice is heard and valued.
* Value-Driven Impact: Join a mission-led organization where our core values—Live for Customer Value, The Best Team Wins, We Own It, and Earth Is Our Future—drive every decision.


**About Us**
 Celonis makes processes work — for people, companies, and the planet. Powered by process mining and AI, the Celonis Process Intelligence Platform integrates process data and business context to create a living digital twin of business operations. We enable thousands of companies worldwide to understand how their business actually runs and, together with their partners, build intelligent solutions that transform and continuously improve the way they operate — unlocking billions in value. Celonis is headquartered in Munich, Germany, and New York City, USA, with more than 20 offices worldwide.
   

  

 Get familiar with the Celonis Process Intelligence Platform by watching this video.
   

  

**Celonis Inclusion Statement**
 At Celonis, we believe our people make us who we are and that “The Best Team Wins”. We know that the best teams are made up of people who bring different perspectives to the table. And when everyone feels included, able to speak up and knows their voice is heard - that's when creativity and innovation happen.
   

  

**Your Privacy**
 Any information you submit to Celonis as part of your application will be processed in accordance with Celonis’ Accessibility and Candidate Notices
   

  

 By submitting this application, you confirm that you agree to the storing and processing of your personal data by Celonis as described in our Privacy Notice for the Application and Hiring Process.
   

  

 Please be aware of common job offer scams, impersonators and frauds. Learn more here.

title: Facilities Supervisor
description:
Job ID
   

  

 278461
   

  

 Posted
   

  

 28-May-2026
   

  

 Service line
   

  

 GWS Segment
   

  

 Role type
   

  

 Full-time
   

  

 Areas of Interest
   

  

 Facilities Management
   

  

 Location(s)
   

  

 Stockholm - Stockholms lan - Sweden
   

  

**Facilities Supervisor**
 Do you want to work as a Facilities Supervisor in a global company with strong local presence and a collaborative culture?
   

  

**About CBRE**
 CBRE is the world's leading company in commercial real estate and property-related services, with more than 140,000 employees globally supporting clients in over 100 countries. In Global Workplace Solutions (GWS), we deliver integrated facility management solutions to some of the world's leading companies.
   

  

 CBRE is a values-driven company guided by our core values RISE: Respect, Integrity, Service and Excellence – values that shape our culture and the way we work together.
   

  

 We are looking for a Facilities Supervisor to join our team. In the role, you will be owning facilities management of the client’s Swedish office while also leading a receptionist team of 2 and ensuring high quality FM services in daily operations.
   

  

 You will have a varied everyday life with both operational, technical, financial and managerial responsibilities in an international environment.
   

  

**The Role**
 As a Facility Supervisor you will be responsible for the operation and maintenance of one site as well as the management of the reception team (2 employees). You will ensure stable and efficient building operations solutions with a focus on quality, processes and procedures.
   

  

 The position is part of the Western Europe and Nordics Regional Team.
   

  

 Client: internation corporate banking.
   

  

**Primary Tasks**
 Ensure efficient operation and maintenance of the office and technical installations in collaboration with client, technicians, suppliers
   

  

 Build and maintain strong client relationships and handle meetings and follow-up on operational issues
   

  

 Regular and consistent administrational works in the client’s system as well as CBRE
   

  

 Responsible for budget, financial follow-up, variance reports for 1 site.
   

  

 Conduct building inspections and ensure quality, compliance and adherence to local authority and client requirements
   

  

 Ensure implementation and follow-up on environmental, health and safety procedures (EHS/QHSE)
   

  

 Responsible for supplier management
   

  

 Identify improvement opportunities, optimize processes and handle daily operational challenges in collaboration with relevant stakeholders
   

  

 People Manager for 2 receptionists. Responsible for arranging external back up when needed
   

  

 Actively contribute to a culture that reflects CBRE's values and principles of cooperation
   

  

**Who You Are**
 We imagine that you are a resilient and solution-oriented professional with experience in facility management or building operations. You thrive in a role with many stakeholders and are able to keep overview in a dynamic everyday life.
   

  

 Able to work independently while adhering to CBRE and client’s procedures and processes
   

  

 Experience with management, coordination of small teams in corporate environment
   

  

 Experience as office manager, facilities tasks, reception, supplier follow ups
   

  

 Good financial understanding and experience with budgets, reporting and follow-up
   

  

 Knowledge of EHS/QHSE and applicable safety and compliance requirements
   

  

 The ability to handle complex issues and communicate professionally with various stakeholders
   

  

 Strong organizational and administrative skills as well as a proactive approach to tasks
   

  

 Calm under pressure
   

  

**General IT Skills And Experience With The Microsoft Excel**
 Interest in learning more about comprehensive electrical and technical matters related to assets in the workspace and server room
   

  

 Fluent in Swedish and English, both spoken and written
   

  

**Nice To Have**
 Tech savvy
   

  

 Passionate problem solver
   

  

 Fluent in another Nordic language
   

  

**What We Offer**
* A stable employer with international reach and strong Nordic presence
* A varied role with the opportunity to become facilities manager
* Competitive benefits according to local market standards
* Hybried working
* Flexible working hours
* 25 holiday days


**Additional Information**
* Scope: Full-time with minimum 3 days in the office. In average once/month evening hours works required which will be compensated in overtime pay.
* Location: Stockholm, in the heart of city center
* Start date: July 2026


**Apply today — selection is ongoing and the position may be filled before the final application date. All candidates must apply through the system in accordance with GDPR requirements**

title: Content Manager at Kry
description:
At Kry, we're transforming healthcare by making it more accessible, personal, and connected. From digital to physical care, we're building a healthcare experience that meets people where they are and supports them throughout their lives.
   




 About the job
 
 We’re looking for a Content Marketing Manager to take ownership of how we communicate across markets, channels and audiences. You won’t just “manage content” you’ll own how Kry communicates and stays relevant. You will create, edit and ship content in multiple formats, across channels, while building structure and consistency in how we work with content globally. You will help us navigate in today’s AI-driven landscape, where the way people search, discover and build trust is rapidly changing.
 



 This is a hands-on, roll-up-your-sleeves role, best suited for someone at a mid-level stage in their career who wants to take ownership and grow.
   




 What you’ll actually do
 * Own the content output across B2C and B2B channels: app, web, blog, CRM, social, case studies, webinars, video and sales materials, list goes on
* Write, produce and ship content across formats and channels. This isn’t a briefing role
* Ensure a clear red thread and consistent voice across all touchpoints
* Navigate medical and legal reviews without losing the human voice
* Work closely with SEO, CRO, CRM and commercial teams to ensure content performs, not just exists
* Continuously test, learn and iterate based on performance and insights
* Help shape how Kry work with content and own how we show up in an AI-driven search and discovery landscape


 To succeed, you’ll likely have
 * 3–5 years of experience in a content marketing or similar role preferably in a fast-moving environment
* Strong writing and storytelling skills across multiple channels
* Curiosity for SEO, search behavior and AI tools
* A hands-on mindset, you’re a doer, not just a briefer
* Fluency in both Swedish and English, French is a plus
* Experience working with healthcare, health-related content or other regulated industries


 What success looks like
 * We publish high-quality content consistently across channels and markets
* Our communication feels clear, human and distinctly Kry
* Content contributes to traffic, engagement, conversions and sales
* Kry builds a strong presence in AI-driven search and discovery


 What we offer
 * A fast-moving, purpose-driven environment where content and communication truly matter
* A collaborative team that cares deeply about both people and quality
* Real impact. Your work will be seen and felt by millions of patients
* A flexible hybrid setup, with regular time together at our Stockholm HQ
* A warm, humble and open culture where feedback is part of how we grow



  





**Join us and shape your future as well as ours!** 




**If you think you’re up to the challenge, come and help us to grow, innovate and develop. A diverse workforce is an absolute necessity for anyone with a vision like ours: to provide great healthcare for everyone. Therefore we celebrate differences and are committed to creating an inclusive environment where all employees can truly belong and thrive.** 




  





**About Kry/Livi** 




**Kry/Livi is the European leader in digital healthcare, allowing patients to see a doctor, nurse, or psychologist online within minutes. We also combine digital with physical care, transforming how millions of patients access healthcare. Headquartered in Stockholm, we are committed to improving the world’s health through innovation, one patient at a time. In Sweden and Norway we are called Kry. In the UK and France, we operate under the name Livi.** 




**Submissions and Questions** 




**Please submit your application by including your CV, LinkedIn, GitHub, or Portfolio (where applicable). No submission will be accepted via email, nor when the job posting has become inactive. All applications must go via the careers site or an official Kry / Livi job post.**

title: Ambassador Program Specialist - Contractor Position
description:
Would you like to work with a product loved by millions and millions of kids all across the world? Do you want to work with a gaming studio who truly believes in the power of play?
   

  

 Toca Boca is looking for a highly proactive and creative Ambassador Program Specialist to join our Marketing team! In this role, you will be the driving force behind the continued evolution of our global Superfans program and the Superfan Hub - our dedicated creator platform designed to inspire, support, and scale our community of brand advocates.
   

  

 If you thrive on turning audience enthusiasm into engaging creator experiences and cultural moments that strengthen our connection with fans worldwide, we want to hear from you!
   

  

**This is a contractor position with an initial contract of 12 months, and we are looking for someone with previous experience working as a contractor.**
**What You’ll Do**
* Empower the community: Lead the strategy, development, and growth of Toca Boca’s global Superfans program and the Superfan Hub platform.
* Spark creativity: Design and launch engaging initiatives, challenges, and community activations that inspire User-Generated Content (UGC) and long-term brand advocacy.
* Build relationships: Foster a welcoming and inspiring environment by building authentic relationships with creators and community members, making them feel recognized and rewarded.
* Drive platform innovation: Develop educational resources (like the Creator Academy), streamline communication flows, and optimize the overall Superfan Hub experience to support sustainable, scalable growth.
* Collaborate cross-functionally: Work closely with Social Media, Community Management, Brand, Legal, and Product teams to align community initiatives with broader marketing goals while ensuring digital safety best practices for youth audiences.
* Analyze and adapt: Leverage community engagement data and UGC performance to refine your strategies and keep Toca Boca at the cutting edge of fandom behaviors and platform trends.


 ,
   

  

**Is This You?**
 You have proven experience in community marketing, ambassador programs, or fandom communities - ideally within gaming, entertainment, lifestyle, or youth-focused brands.
   

  

 You have a deep understanding of internet culture, online communities, and digital trends across TikTok, YouTube, and Instagram.
   

  

 You have a "hit the ground running" mentality. You independently drive initiatives from concept through launch, taking strong ownership of your work in a fast-moving environment.
   

  

 You possess strong creative instincts to identify culturally relevant opportunities, balanced with the organizational skills to manage multiple priorities and stakeholders.
   

  

 You have excellent communication skills and a genuine passion for creators, entertainment, and community-driven marketing.
   

  

 You believe in the Power of Play!
   

  

**Good to know!**
 This is a contractor position with an initial contract of 12 months. It is based in our Stockholm office with a hybrid working model (part office, part working from home). You need to be located in Sweden as we are unable to support relocation or offer remote work. As we’re an international company, with over 20 nationalities working here, and millions of users all over the world, you have to be fluent in English. Your application should therefore be in English.

title: Regulatory Specialist
description:
We’re looking for a
 **Regulatory Affairs professional** 
 who enjoys working close to the business, not just documenting it.
   

  

 At Boule, QA/RA plays an important role in supporting the organization, helping balance compliance with speed, enabling informed decisions, and contributing to sustainable growth.
   

  

 This role combines Regulatory Affairs with a strong QA perspective, contributing to an efficient and risk-based QMS and enabling sound decision-making across the organisation.
   

  

 You’ll be expected to translate requirements into workable solutions, navigate complexity, and help the organisation move forward in a compliant and practical way.
   

  

**What You’ll Do**
* Drive regulatory activities across markets for our instruments and reagents (human and veterinary)
* Contribute to regulatory strategies and product lifecycle management
* Support and provide documentation for global registrations via distributors and partners
* Work closely with R&D, Operations, and Commercial teams
* Interface with Notified Bodies and regulatory authorities regarding submissions, substantial system changes, and new product developments
* Review design dossiers, technical documentation, and labeling to support regulatory approvals
* Stay informed on regulatory updates and changes, ensuring proactive and compliant implementation
* Translate regulatory requirements into clear, practical, and business-relevant guidance
* Contribute to an efficient and risk-based QMS, with focus on usability and value
* Challenge and simplify processes where possible
* Apply pragmatic, risk-based thinking in decision-making, ensuring compliance while supporting efficient and timely execution


**We think you bring**
* minimum 3+ years experience in Regulatory Affairs within MedTech/IVD
* Understanding of both devices and consumables/reagents
* Familiarity with relevant technical and product safety areas (e.g. electrical safety, EMC, laser safety (IEC 60825), and chemical regulations such as CLP/GHS)
* Understanding of software-related regulatory requirements, including awareness of software lifecycle, validation, and cybersecurity considerations in a medical device context
* Experience working in a QMS (ISO 13485)
* Experience working with international distributors and global markets
* Experience with veterinary products is an advantage
* Fluent in English and Swedish


**Most importantly**
 You are pragmatic and structured, with the ability to apply risk-based thinking in complex situations.
   

  

 You work closely with the business and can translate regulatory requirements into practical solutions, ensuring compliance while supporting progress and efficiency.
   

  

**What We Offer**
 At Boule, you will be part of an engaged team with short decision-making paths and plenty of room for your own initiatives.
   

  

 In this role, you will work with established products in a global context, with a central responsibility in navigating complex international flows and collaborating closely with colleagues and distributors worldwide. You will also have good opportunities to influence and improve our processes.
   

  

 We value a safe and sustainable work environment with strong employment conditions and therefore offer the possibility of hybrid work. Boule is covered by the Swedish collective bargaining agreements (Teknikavtalen). For salaried employees, this includes agreements with Unionen, Sveriges Ingenjörer, and Ledarna, and for blue-collar employees, IF Metall.
   

  

 The position is located in Spånga, just outside Stockholm.
   

  

**Recruitment process**
 If your profile matches the role, we will invite you to an initial interview with HR. This is followed by more in-depth meetings with relevant department stakeholders, in a process where your potential role is central.
   

  

**Application**
 Welcome to submit your application no later than May 29. Selection is ongoing, so please don’t wait to apply.
   

  

 If you have any questions, you are warmly welcome to contact the HR department at: hr@boule.se
   

  

 Welcome to become part of Boule — we look forward to your application!
   

  

**About The Company**
 Boule Diagnostics AB is a global diagnostics company that develops, manufactures, and sells systems and consumables for blood cell counting (hematology). The company primarily serves small and medium-sized hospitals, clinics, and laboratories, as well as other diagnostics companies within both human and veterinary hematology.
   

  

 Operations are conducted through subsidiaries in Sweden, the USA, Mexico, and other locations. Sales are global, primarily via distributors, supported by Boule’s own local sales and service personnel.
   

  

 Since 2011, Boule Diagnostics has been listed on Nasdaq Stockholm. As a leading player in decentralized hematology diagnostics, Boule is known for high quality, user-friendly products, and a strong international presence.
   

  

 In 2025, Boule Diagnostics was included in Allbright’s Green List and recognized as one of Sweden’s most gender-equal companies — an award that reflects our continued focus on an inclusive and balanced workplace.

title: Staff Software Engineer
description:
**The Company**
 PayPal has been revolutionizing commerce globally for more than 25 years. Creating innovative experiences that make moving money, selling, and shopping simple, personalized, and secure, PayPal empowers consumers and businesses in approximately 200 markets to join and thrive in the global economy.
   

  

 We operate a global, two-sided network at scale that connects hundreds of millions of merchants and consumers. We help merchants and consumers connect, transact, and complete payments, whether they are online or in person. PayPal is more than a connection to third-party payment networks. We provide proprietary payment solutions accepted by merchants that enable the completion of payments on our platform on behalf of our customers.
   

  

 We offer our customers the flexibility to use their accounts to purchase and receive payments for goods and services, as well as the ability to transfer and withdraw funds. We enable consumers to exchange funds more safely with merchants using a variety of funding sources, which may include a bank account, a PayPal or Venmo account balance, PayPal and Venmo branded credit products, a credit card, a debit card, certain cryptocurrencies, or other stored value products such as gift cards, and eligible credit card rewards. Our PayPal, Venmo, and Xoom products also make it safer and simpler for friends and family to transfer funds to each other. We offer merchants an end-to-end payments solution that provides authorization and settlement capabilities, as well as instant access to funds and payouts. We also help merchants connect with their customers, process exchanges and returns, and manage risk. We enable consumers to engage in cross-border shopping and merchants to extend their global reach while reducing the complexity and friction involved in enabling cross-border trade.
   

  

 Our beliefs are the foundation for how we conduct business every day. We live each day guided by our core values of Inclusion, Innovation, Collaboration, and Wellness. Together, our values ensure that we work together as one global team with our customers at the center of everything we do – and they push us to ensure we take care of ourselves, each other, and our communities.
   

  

**Job Summary:**
**What You Need To Know About The Role**
 In this role, you will be leading the transformation of products and services powering in-store merchant run the business experience towards a unified omni experience. This means not only driving customer experience cohesion, but also converging the underlying data and service layer towards the ones powering online experience.
   

  

 You'll be working with both technical stakeholders - Engineering Managers, Senior Engineers, skip-level leaders - and other functions (Product Managers, Customer Support, Sales) to evolve and adjust the technical direction in order to meet the business objectives.
   

  

**Job Description:**
**Essential Responsibilities:**
* Acts as a project or system leader, coordinating the activities of other engineers on the project or within the system
* Determines the technical tasks that other engineers will follow
* Actions result in the achievement of customer, operational, program or service objectives
* Proactively improves existing structures & processes
* Exercises judgement in reconciling diverse and competing priorities (time, quality, complexity risk) to identify optimal solutions to problems
* Notices patterns and condenses repetition into densely meaningful generalized solutions
* Collaborates with management to set/improve standards for engineering rigor
* Trusted to represent PayPal, Inc to the outside world via interactions with partners, customers, or industry organizations


**Minimum Qualifications:**
* 5+ years relevant experience and a Bachelor’s degree OR Any equivalent combination of education and experience.


**Additional Responsibilities & Preferred Qualifications:**
**Your day-to-day**
 As a Staff Software Engineer, you’ll be interacting with multiple teams owning the Point of Sale offering. You will be aligning the technical direction with the business problem, questioning the requirements, and challenging the status quo by ensuring that technical decisions align with business direction - both the ones currently being made, but also revising the historical ones as needed.
   

  

**Your day-to-day will primarily involve:**
* Guiding the principles and considerations behind designing and developing scalable backend systems to support and enable engineering teams you work with.
* Identifying opportunities for strengthening technical cohesion while reducing intentional and incidental coupling between systems
* Proactively identifying architectural and execution challenges and ensuring leadership visibility and follow-up on them
* Mentoring other engineers while growing own competence


**What You Need To Succeed**
* Proven background in large-scale, high-performance system design and optimization.
* Effective communication with both technical and non-technical audiences.
* Experience leading informally through expert influence and building partnerships.
* Advanced proficiency in Java, with strong expertise in core concepts, design patterns, and optimization techniques.
* Extensive hands-on experience with Java frameworks like Spring (Spring Boot, Spring MVC) and Hibernate, building scalable and maintainable solutions.
* Strong ability to collaborate with and influence cross-functional teams, contributing to technical leadership through code reviews, design discussions, and mentoring junior engineers.
* Deep knowledge of cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, Azure), utilizing them to deliver cost-effective and scalable solutions.
* Solid understanding of both relational and NoSQL databases, with hands-on experience in ORM tools like Hibernate to ensure efficient data handling and performance tuning.


**Get bonus points if you also have...**
* Knowledge of the payment processing industry and relevant regulations.
* Contributions to open-source projects.
* Familiarity with microservices architecture and containerization (Docker, Kubernetes).


**Subsidiary:**
 PayPal
   

  

**Travel Percent:**
 0
   

  

 PayPal does not charge candidates any fees for courses, applications, resume reviews, interviews, background checks, or onboarding. When making an application directly, we will never ask you to share passwords, one-time passcodes (OTP), or verification codes. Any such request is a red flag and likely part of a scam. All communication regarding your application will come from official PayPal email domains. If you suspect fraudulent activity, please report it immediately. To learn more about how to identify and avoid recruitment fraud please visit https://careers.pypl.com/contact-us.
   

  

 For the majority of employees, PayPal's balanced hybrid work model offers 3 days in the office for effective in-person collaboration and 2 days at your choice of either the PayPal office or your home workspace, ensuring that you equally have the benefits and conveniences of both locations.
   

  

**Our Benefits:**
 At PayPal, we’re committed to building an equitable and inclusive global economy. And we can’t do this without our most important asset-you. That’s why we offer comprehensive, choice-based programs, to support all aspects of personal wellbeing—physical, emotional, and financial—delivering meaningful value where it matters most. We strive to create a flexible, balanced work culture with a holistic approach to benefits, including generous paid time off, healthcare coverage for you and your family, and resources to create financial security and support your mental health.
   

  

**Who We Are:**
 Click Here to learn more about our culture and community.
   

  

**Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion**
 PayPal provides equal employment opportunity (EEO) to all persons regardless of age, color, national origin, citizenship status, physical or mental disability, race, religion, creed, gender, sex, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity and/or expression, genetic information, marital status, status with regard to public assistance, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law. In addition, PayPal will provide reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities. If you are unable to submit an application because of incompatible assistive technology or a disability, please contact us at paypalglobaltalentacquisition@paypal.com.
   

  

**Belonging at PayPal:**
 Our employees are central to advancing our mission, and we strive to create an environment where everyone can do their best work with a sense of purpose and belonging. Belonging at PayPal means creating a workplace with a sense of acceptance and security where all employees feel included and valued. We are proud to have a diverse workforce reflective of the merchants, consumers, and communities that we serve, and we continue to take tangible actions to cultivate inclusivity and belonging at PayPal.
   

  

 Any general requests for consideration of your skills, please Join our Talent Community.
   

  

 We know the confidence gap and imposter syndrome can get in the way of meeting spectacular candidates. Please don’t hesitate to apply.

title: Senior Marketing Manager
description:
****Join us to shape the future!****
 Teamtailor is a global Employer Branding and ATS SaaS platform used by over 12,000 companies, 250,000 users, and available in 90 countries worldwide. 🌍
   

  

 Working at Teamtailor means being part of a dynamic, fast-paced tech company that values impact and responsibility. Our workplace fosters an environment where everyone can contribute meaningfully to the growth of the company. 🎉🥳
   

  

 Building a diverse team with a wide range of experiences, skills, and backgrounds has always been central to Teamtailor’s mission, and it remains a strength today. We welcome all individuals who share our vision and are committed to contributing to our collective success. Join us in helping companies and people communicate in more meaningful ways to make life-changing decisions together.👫
   

  

 As we continue to grow, we’re looking for a
 **Senior Marketing Manager** 
 to help us strengthen the connection between marketing and sales and drive pipeline growth across strategic accounts and markets.
   

  

 This is a highly commercial role at the intersection of:
   

  

* account-based marketing
* field marketing
* pipeline generation
* pipeline acceleration
* sales collaboration


 You’ll lead a growing team focused on generating and accelerating pipeline while creating standout experiences together with Brand, Creative, and Performance Marketing.
   

  

 Reporting directly to the CMO, you’ll play a key role in shaping how marketing contributes to Teamtailor’s continued growth.
   

  

**Own pipeline generation and acceleration!**
* Develop and execute the strategy for pipeline growth across markets and segments
* Partner closely with sales leadership on pipeline priorities and GTM initiatives
* Build scalable programs that generate demand and help move opportunities forward


**Lead ABM and strategic account programs!**
* Drive account-based marketing initiatives for target accounts and segments
* Create coordinated multi-touch programs across sales and marketing
* Increase engagement across buying committees and strategic accounts


**Own field marketing and experiences!**
* Lead field marketing initiatives including executive events, customer roundtables, dinners, webinars, and regional activations
* Ensure all programs are designed to generate or accelerate pipeline
* Collaborate closely with Brand & Creative to create memorable experiences and campaigns that stand out in the market


**Drive growth together with Performance Marketing!**
* Partner closely with the Performance Marketing team to scale demand generation efforts and drive pipeline growth
* Align account targeting, campaigns, and conversion initiatives across paid and owned channels
* Ensure enterprise and strategic marketing motions are supported by scalable acquisition programs


**Own webinar execution!**
* Build scalable webinar programs targeted toward key audiences and buying personas
* Collaborate with Product Marketing, Customer Marketing, and Sales on topics, speakers, and follow-up strategies
* Ensure webinars contribute to pipeline generation and account engagement


**Partner marketing and ecosystem growth!**
* Work with strategic partners on co-marketing initiatives and pipeline-generating activities
* Help expand Teamtailor’s presence through ecosystem relationships and regional partnerships


**Measure impact!**
* Own KPIs tied to pipeline generation, account engagement, opportunity creation, and pipeline influence
* Continuously analyze performance and optimize programs based on commercial outcomes


**Lead and develop the team!**
* Manage and support a team consisting of local field marketing and partner marketing experts across markets
* Create strong alignment between marketing and regional sales teams
* Help scale the function as Teamtailor continues to grow


**Who you are!**
 We believe you’re a commercially minded marketer who thrives in the space between marketing and sales.
   

  

 You understand how modern B2B buying works and know how to build programs that create momentum with strategic accounts and sales teams.
   

  

**You Likely Have**
* Experience working closely with sales teams in a B2B SaaS environment
* Strong background in ABM, field marketing, strategic demand generation, or pipeline marketing
* Experience generating and accelerating B2B SaaS pipeline
* Experience running webinars, executive events, and account-focused programs
* A strong understanding of multi-stakeholder buying journeys
* A data-driven and commercially focused mindset
* The ability to operate both strategically and hands-on
* Strong collaboration and stakeholder management skills
* Experience leading and developing teams
* You combine creativity with strong commercial and analytical thinking
* You’re curious about how AI is reshaping modern marketing and GTM teams
* You enjoy building workflows, automations, and scalable systems using AI and modern tools
* You’re comfortable experimenting, iterating, and finding smarter ways to generate and accelerate pipeline


**Bonus If You Have Experience From**
* HR tech
* SaaS
* scaling international B2B companies


 If this sounds like you, please hit apply and get your potential journey with Teamtailor started!

title: Tech Lead till ID06
description:
Vill du ta ett större ansvar för teknikval, arkitektur och teamets utveckling och samtidigt bidra till ett viktigt samhällsuppdrag?
   

  

 ID06 driver affärer som bidrar till att minska arbetskraftskriminalitet och fusk i Sverige. Vårt uppdrag är att främja sund konkurrens, motverka svartarbete och ekonomisk brottslighet samt bidra till schyssta villkor på arbetsmarknaden.
   

  

 Vi söker nu en Tech Lead som vill kombinera djup teknisk kompetens med ett ledande ansvar och vara med och utveckla lösningar som gör verklig skillnad för arbetsmarknaden.
   

  

 Rollen är både strategisk och operativ med stort tekniskt ansvar. Som Tech Lead hos oss får du en nyckelroll i att forma och vidareutveckla våra egenutvecklade digitala tjänster från arkitektur och design till implementation och drift.
   

  

 Du leder det tekniska arbetet i teamet, coachar kollegor och säkerställer att vi bygger robusta, säkra och skalbara lösningar i en modern molnmiljö (Microsoft Azure).
   

  

 Du arbetar nära produktägare, verksamhet och andra utvecklare för att omsätta affärsbehov till hållbara och genomtänkta tekniska lösningar.
   

  

 Rollen ger dig stort mandat att påverka teknikval, arbetssätt, processer och att vara en teknisk förebild som driver kvalitet, säkerhet och innovation framåt.
   

  

**Du kommer att:**
* Leda och coacha utvecklingsteamet i tekniska frågor och best practice samt även vara aktiv i utvecklingsarbetet
* Ansvara för systemarkitektur, designbeslut och teknisk roadmap
* Säkerställa kodkvalitet, testbarhet och stabila leveranser
* Driva teknisk förbättring, automatisering och utveckling av DevOps-processer
* Omvärldsbevaka ny teknik och initiera relevanta förbättringar
* Vara ett tekniskt bollplank till produktägare och bidra i prioritering och planering
* Delta aktivt i rekrytering och kompetensutveckling


**Därför kommer du att trivas hos oss**
 Hos oss blir du en del av en inkluderande och framåtblickande organisation där du får stort inflytande, möjlighet att växa och chansen att bidra till något som verkligen gör skillnad.
   

  

 Vi erbjuder bland annat:
   

  

* En strategisk och operativ roll med stort mandat
* Möjlighet att arbeta med hela kedjan - från idé till produktion
* En modern, molnbaserad miljö med fokus på kvalitet, säkerhet och automation
* Kompetenta och engagerade kollegor i ett öppet och prestigelöst klimat
* Kontinuerlig kompetensutveckling och stöd för certifieringar
* Moderna kontor centralt i Stockholm, Göteborg och Jönköping
* Hybrid- och distansarbete
* Kollektivavtal (ITP1), arbetstidsförkortning, sjukvårdsförsäkring och friskvårdsbidrag
* Ett bolag med en viktig roll i samhället


**Det här söker vi hos dig**
 För att lyckas i rollen har du flera års erfarenhet av systemutveckling i moderna teknologier, exempelvis .NET, Node.js, Python, Java, React och Azure.
   

  

 Vi ser att du:
   

  

* Har dokumenterad erfarenhet av att leda team, projekt eller tekniska initiativ
* Är van att ta ansvar för arkitektur, teknikval och leverans
* Har god förståelse för molnbaserad utveckling och CI/CD
* Behärskar svenska mycket väl, i tal och skrift
* Är en trygg problemlösare och kommunikativ ledare
* Trivs i tvärfunktionellt samarbete nära affären
* Har ett starkt intresse för ny teknik och att driva förbättring


**Ansökan och information**
 I denna rekrytering samarbetar ID06 med Experis IT. Vid frågor är du välkommen att kontakta ansvarig rekryteringskonsult Anki Snis på 08-508 954 53 eller via e-post: anki.snis@se.experis.com.
   

  

 Omfattning: Heltid. Vi tillämpar provanställning.
   

  

 Säkerhetskontroll genomförs före anställning.
   

  

 Intervjuer sker löpande - skicka därför gärna in din ansökan så snart som möjligt.
   

  

 Varmt välkommen med din ansökan!
   

  

**Om oss**
 ID06 utvecklar, säljer och förvaltar lösningar som säkerställer att företag och deras medarbetare har rätt att arbeta i Sverige och har lämnat korrekta uppgifter. Genom att motarbeta svartarbete, arbetslivskriminalitet och ekonomisk brottslighet bidrar vi till sund konkurrens och att alla spelar efter samma regler - i bygg- och fastighetssektorn och i andra branscher.
   

  

 ID06 AB grundades 2006 av Byggföretagen med målet att främja sund konkurrens och säkra arbetsplatser.
   

  

 Sedan starten har vårt fokus varit bygg- och fastighetsbranschen. Nu utvecklar vi våra digitala tjänster vidare och möter behov i fler branscher där kraven på kontroll av identitet, medborgarskap och rätten att arbeta i Sverige ökar.

title: Data Engineer Consultant with Databricks experience
description:
**Locations:** 
 Stockholm (or Helsinki or Munich)
   

  

**Start Date:** 
 August 2026
   

  

**The opportunity**
 Join our Data & Analytics team as a Data Engineer. You won't just move data, you’ll get to build the scalable pipelines that drive our client projects and strategic decisions.
   

  

 This role is a recruitment for a specific long-term assignment at one of our most forward leaning clients who are working with Databricks as part of their architecture. The goal of our partnership is to drive growth across their commercial operations with the help of AI and automation. We are developing cutting edge AI and agentic use cases and solutions in the client’s enterprise environment in a cross-functional team consisting of business developers, data scientists, analysts, data engineers and marketing and sales channel specialists.
   

  

**Your toolkit**
 We are looking for a builder who thrives in a modern cloud-data environment, and has experience working as a consultant delivering business results to clients via data.
   

  

* The Core: 3–5 years of Data Engineering experience with a degree in CS, Math, or Physics.
* Databricks Expert: At least 3 years of hands-on experience orchestrating workloads within Databricks.
* The Languages: Proficient in Python and Apache Spark for complex data processing.
* Data Design: Proven experience designing and implementing data platforms using Databricks, Apache Spark, SQL and Python.
* Visualization: Ability to bridge the gap between raw data and insights through dashboard development.
* Communication: Fluency in English is required and depending on office location it is great if you also know the local language to some extent (Swedish, Finnish or German).


**Why join us?**
 Our vision is to become Europe's leading actionable marketing data-asset provider. We believe companies must invest in intelligence/AI, customer experience, and change management to survive. Our mission is to foster customer-centric organizations equipped for future growth, heavily driven by smart data and analytics.
   

  

 In today's fast-evolving market, companies need strong data capabilities. We utilize cutting-edge cloud technologies and machine learning to build scalable, future-proof data solutions as consultants.
   

  

 At Avaus, you will find a collaborative environment valuing personal responsibility, freedom, problem-solving, and continuous learning. We seek individuals who take ownership, drive initiatives, and enjoy mutual learning.
   

  

**Practicalities**
***Probation***
 : 6-month initial period.
   

  

***Process***
 : We review applications continuously, so the sooner you apply, the better!
   

  

***Privacy***
 : To keep your data safe (GDPR), we only accept applications via our career portal.

title: Staff Systems Engineer (C++) - Remote Work | REF#289957
description:
At BairesDev®, we've been leading the way in technology projects for over 15 years. We deliver cutting-edge solutions to giants like Google and the most innovative startups in Silicon Valley.
   

  

 Our diverse 4,000+ team, composed of the world's Top 1% of tech talent, works remotely on roles that drive significant impact worldwide.
   

  

 When you apply for this position, you're taking the first step in a process that goes beyond the ordinary. We aim to align your passions and skills with our vacancies, setting you on a path to exceptional career development and success.
   

  

 Staff Systems Engineer (C++) at BairesDev
   

  

 We’re looking for a Staff Systems Engineer with expert-level C++ skills and deep experience in the internals of distributed query engines. You’ve gone beyond writing performant code — you’ve defined how performance is measured, led optimization efforts across teams, and built the benchmarking infrastructure that makes that work possible. You’ll be taking on a senior IC role on query engine infrastructure that powers analytics for some of the largest data environments in the world, shaping technical direction and setting the standard for how the team approaches performance at scale.
   

  

**What You’ll Do**
* Lead the design and optimization of critical query engine components in C++ for distributed analytics infrastructure.
* Define and drive CPU-level performance tuning strategies across the execution engine.
* Build and own benchmarking frameworks to measure and track performance at cluster scale.
* Set technical direction for distributed query performance across engineering teams.
* Mentor engineers and lead cross-team initiatives focused on query execution and optimization.
* Partner with stakeholders to prioritize and deliver high-impact performance improvements.


**What We Are Looking For**
* 7+ years of experience in IT roles.
* 5+ years of experience with C++ as a primary programming language.
* Deep expertise in execution engine and query optimizer internals.
* Proven experience with CPU-level performance tuning in production environments.
* Background in benchmarking strategy and cluster-scale performance measurement.
* Demonstrated ability to lead cross-team optimization initiatives and influence technical direction.
* Advanced proficiency in English.


 How we do make your work (and your life) easier:
   

  

* Remote Work.
* Excellent compensation in USD or your local currency if preferred
* Hardware and software setup for you to work from home.
* Flexible hours: create your own schedule.
* Paid parental leaves, vacations, and national holidays.
* Innovative and multicultural work environment: collaborate and learn from the global Top 1% of talent.
* Supportive environment with mentorship, promotions, skill development, and diverse growth opportunities.


 Join a global team where your unique talents can truly thrive and make a significant impact!
   

  

 Apply now!
   

  

 #BD-PRIO-2026

title: Sektionschef – Arkiv, utveckling och förvaltning Regeringskansliet
description:
**RK Informationsförvaltning, Förvaltningsavdelningen**
 Vill du leda och utveckla Regeringskansliets arkivverksamhet och bidra till rättssäker hantering av allmänna handlingar? Hos oss får du kombinera operativt ledarskap med strategisk utveckling av arbetssätt, digitala stöd och styrning – i en miljö med höga krav på kvalitet, säkerhet och service.
   

  

**Din arbetsdag. Sveriges morgondag.**
 Arbetet som chef för sektionen arkiv, utveckling och förvaltning på Regeringskansliet innebär att du planerar, följer upp, samordnar och företräder verksamheten. Du ansvarar för den dagliga driften av verksamheten, samt leder och driver utvecklingen inom sektionens verksamhetsområde. Rollen som sektionschef omfattar personalansvar (ca 12 medarbetare) vilket bl.a. innebär att hålla planerings-, uppföljnings- och lönesamtal samt göra utvecklingsplaner för medarbetarna. Du har budgetansvar och följer löpande upp det ekonomiska utfallet, gör prognos och säkerställer att resultatet följer enhetens samlade budget. Som sektionschef ingår du i enhetens ledningsgrupp där du bidrar till enhetens gemensamma mål och utveckling.
   

  

 Sektionen ansvarar för Regeringskansliets arkivverksamhet. I detta ingår bland annat vård och förvaltning av arkivbestånden, upprättande av styrdokument på området, uppmärksamma och driva utvecklingsinsatser samt ge råd och stöd till myndigheten, utlandsmyndigheterna och kommittéer. Sektionen har också ansvar för tillsyn över utlandsmyndigheternas arkiv och diarieföring. Sektionen ansvarar för den verksamhetsnära förvaltningen och utvecklingen av verksamhetsområdets IT-stöd.
   

  

 Läs mer om vår verksamhet på www.regeringen.se.
   

  

**Din bakgrund**
 Vi söker dig som har högskoleexamen i arkiv- och informationsvetenskap eller annan jämförlig utbildning som arbetsgivaren bedömer relevant. Du har flerårig erfarenhet av arbete som chef med ansvar för personal, budget och arbetsmiljö inom arkiv- och/eller registraturverksamhet i offentlig sektor. Du har gedigen erfarenhet av digital informationshantering med särskilt fokus på arkiv- och registraturprocesser samt dokumenterad vana av att arbeta med förändringsledning och verksamhetsutveckling. Rollen kräver mycket god kunskap om den lagstiftning som reglerar allmänna handlingar, arkivhandlingar samt offentlighet och sekretess. Vidare har du strategisk kompetens inom informationsförvaltning, exempelvis när det gäller styrning av arkivbildning, klassificeringsstrukturer och framtagande av styrdokument. Du har även kunskap om och erfarenhet av informationssäkerhet kopplad till allmänna handlingar, med särskilt fokus på hantering av skyddsvärd information.
   

  

 Det är meriterande om du har genomgått relevanta ledarskapsutbildningar samt har utbildning inom projektledning. Det är även meriterande om du har erfarenhet av att arbeta som projektledare och har deltagit i eller ansvarat för utveckling och förvaltning av IT‑stöd, gärna i en verksamhetsnära och komplex organisation.
   

  

 Mycket goda kunskaper i svenska och goda kunskaper i engelska, i tal och skrift, är ett krav.
   

  

**Dina egenskaper**
 Som chef på Regeringskansliet har du helhetssyn och kommunicerar övergripande mål, involverar andra tidigt och främjar samarbete för en enhetlig ledning. Du driver utveckling av verksamhet och arbetsmiljö, leder förändring med mod och uppmuntrar lärande och initiativ till utveckling, samtidigt som du utvecklar din egen kompetens och ditt ledarskap. Du har fokus på resultat genom att prioritera och fatt beslut, visar tillit till medarbetares kompetens genom att stötta och coacha samt hanterar problem i tid. I denna roll värdesätter vi särskilt att du är trygg och stabil samt att din samarbetsförmåga är stark. Du bygger förtroendefulla relationer, samarbetar effektivt över organisatoriska gränser och hanterar olikheter och meningsskiljaktigheter på ett konstruktivt sätt.
   

  

 Läs mer på: Medarbetarskap och ledarskap i Regeringskansliet - Regeringen.se
   

  

**Övrigt**
 Tillsvidareanställning (med sex månaders inledande provanställning) samt ett tidsbegränsat förordnande som chef. Tillträde enligt överenskommelse. Befattningen är placerad i säkerhetsklass och en säkerhetsprövning med registerkontroll kommer att genomföras innan beslut om anställning fattas. Du måste vara svensk medborgare, men får gärna ha utländsk bakgrund
   

  

**Är du intresserad?**
 Vill du veta mer om jobbet och vad vi erbjuder, kontakta enhetschef Jenny Savonen. Du är också välkommen att kontakta rekryteringsspecialist Siri Hågefalk. Fackliga kontaktpersoner är Johan Strokirk för Saco och Jan Wahlström för ST. Du når samtliga via Regeringskansliets växel, 08-405 10 00. Vi tar gärna emot samtal från dig som är intresserad av jobbet men vi tackar nej till dig som säljer annonser och rekryteringstjänster.
   

  

 Välkommen att registrera din ansökan i vårt rekryteringsverktyg
 **senast den 2:a juni 2026.**
 I ansökningsformuläret ingår urvalsfrågor kopplade till skallkrav och meriter. Du lämnar korta, verifierbara uppgifter (t.ex. examen, arbetsgivare och tidsperiod). Det tar cirka 5–10 minuter och underlättar en effektiv och rättssäker urvalsprocess enligt LOA.
   

  

 Regeringskansliets uppdrag är att stödja regeringen i dess arbete med att styra Sverige och förverkliga sin politik. Här arbetar 4 800 personer, varav 600 utomlands. Regeringskansliet välkomnar sökande med olika bakgrund och erfarenheter. Vi ser jämställdhet och mångfald som en styrka och tillgång och vi arbetar aktivt för att vara en arbetsplats fri från diskriminering. Förvaltningsavdelningen ansvarar för myndighetsövergripande frågor som förändrings- och förnyelsearbete, ekonomi, arbetsgivarfrågor, kompetensförsörjning, kommunikation, säkerhet, it, bibliotek, arkiv och registratur samt för den gemensamma servicen i Regeringskansliet.

title: Ungdomshandledare till Framtidens Hus
description:
Välkommen till oss
   

  

 Enheten för Framtidens hus ingår i avdelningen för främjande och förebyggande sociala insatser i Järva stadsdelsförvaltning. Enheten består idag av Framtidens Hus Husby, Framtidens hus i Rinkeby och snart öppnar en Framtidens hus i Tensta. Framtidens hus arbetar utifrån visionen ”Alla ungdomars arena för att förverkliga sina drömmar och bygga framtidstro”.
   

  

 Verksamheten ska attrahera ungdomar i åldrarna 16-19 år och har en inriktning mot studier, arbete, musik, idrott, IT/tech samt kultur och drama. Verksamheten förekommer under kvällar och helger och det är därför viktigt att du trivs med ett varierat schema.
   

  

 Vi erbjuder
   

  

 Som medarbetare hos oss erbjuds du bland annat friskvårdsbidrag, rabatterat kort till stadens sim- och gym-anläggningar, kompetensutveckling samt tillgång till en lärportal med kurser och föreläsningar. Du omfattas även av tjänstepension enligt kollektivavtal och förmånliga försäkringar. Läs gärna mer om Stockholms stads förmåner här,
   

  

 Din roll
   

  

 Som ungdomshandledare med inriktning studier och arbete arbetar du med att skapa möjligheter för unga att utforska vägar till arbete, studier och eget skapande. Du stödjer ungdomars utveckling och ger vägledning och inspiration inför framtida studier och yrkesval.
   

  

**Arbetsuppgifter:**
